description: Add CLI to fitzRoy-ts exposing all library functions as terminal commands with table, JSON, and CSV output

userStories.title: Add CLI dependencies and package.json config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the CLI dependencies installed and package.json updated so that the CLI infrastructure is ready.
acceptanceCriteria: Add citty, @clack/prompts, and picocolors as runtime dependencies
Add bunup as a dev dependency
Add bin field: { "fitzroy": "./dist/cli.js" }
Add files field: ["dist"]
Add engines field: { "node": ">=20" }
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create CLI entry point and root command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want a root fitzroy command with --version and --help so that I can discover available subcommands.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/cli.ts with shebang (#!/usr/bin/env node) as the CLI entry point
Define root fitzroy command using Citty with description and version from package.json
fitzroy --version prints the package version
fitzroy --help displays help text listing all subcommands
CLI entry point is NOT importable from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create output format dispatcher and JSON formatter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a format dispatcher that picks the correct formatter based on flags so that all commands share formatting logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/cli/formatters/json.ts that pretty-prints data as JSON via JSON.stringify
Create src/cli/formatters/index.ts as the format dispatcher
Dispatcher selects format based on --json, --csv, --format flags
When stdout is not a TTY (piped), default to JSON instead of table
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create CSV formatter
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want --csv output so that I can import data into spreadsheets or pipe to cut.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/cli/formatters/csv.ts that outputs CSV with a header row
Properly escape fields containing commas, quotes, or newlines
Handle arrays of flat objects (MatchResult[], PlayerStats[], etc.)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create table formatter
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want human-readable table output as the default format so that data is easy to scan in the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/cli/formatters/table.ts with hand-rolled padded column formatting
Truncate output to terminal width (process.stdout.columns)
Accept a columns config to show a subset of fields by default
Support --full flag to show all columns
No external table library dependency
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create UI helpers for spinner and summary
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want a spinner during data fetching and a summary line after so that I get interactive feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/cli/ui.ts wrapping @clack/prompts spinner
Export withSpinner() helper that shows a spinner while an async operation runs
Export showSummary() that prints a summary line (e.g. 'Loaded 198 matches for 2025')
Suppress spinners and interactive output when stdout is not a TTY
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create matches subcommand
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want fitzroy matches --season 2025 so that I can fetch match results from the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/cli/commands/matches.ts using Citty defineCommand
Required arg: --season. Optional: --round, --source (default: afl-api), --competition (default: AFLM)
Calls fetchMatchResults from the library and formats output via dispatcher
Default table shows: date, round, home team, away team, home score, away score, venue
Shows spinner during fetch, summary line after
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create stats subcommand
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want fitzroy stats --season 2025 so that I can fetch player statistics from the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/cli/commands/stats.ts using Citty defineCommand
Required arg: --season. Optional: --round, --match-id, --source, --competition
Calls fetchPlayerStats from the library and formats output via dispatcher
Default table shows: player name, team, disposals, kicks, handballs, marks, goals
Shows spinner during fetch, summary line after
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create fixture and ladder subcommands
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want fitzroy fixture and fitzroy ladder subcommands so that I can view schedules and standings.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/cli/commands/fixture.ts — required: --season, optional: --round, --source, --competition
Create src/cli/commands/ladder.ts — required: --season, optional: --round, --source, --competition
Both call their respective library functions and format via dispatcher
Fixture table shows: round, date, home, away, venue
Ladder table shows: position, team, wins, losses, draws, percentage, points
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create lineup, squad, and teams subcommands
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want fitzroy lineup, squad, and teams subcommands so that I can view rosters and team lists.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/cli/commands/lineup.ts — required: --season, --round, optional: --match-id, --source, --competition
Create src/cli/commands/squad.ts — required: --team-id, --season, optional: --competition
Create src/cli/commands/teams.ts — optional: --competition, --team-type
Each calls its library function and formats via dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CLI error handling and exit codes
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want clear error messages without stack traces so that I can understand what went wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: Wrap all command execution in a top-level error boundary in src/cli.ts
Catch Zod validation errors and display formatted messages with the invalid field and issue
Catch AflApiError, ScrapeError, and UnsupportedSourceError with human-readable messages
Exit with code 1 on error, code 0 on success
Never show raw stack traces — show the error message only, with colour via picocolors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build pipeline with bunup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a build pipeline that produces the library bundle, CLI bundle, and type declarations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create bunup.config.ts with dual entry points (src/index.ts, src/cli.ts)
ESM-only output format
Generate .d.ts for the library entry only
Add scripts: build (bunup), prepublishOnly (bun run build)
bun run build produces dist/index.js, dist/index.d.ts, and dist/cli.js
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CLI argument validation tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for CLI argument Zod schemas to verify validation works correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/cli/ directory for CLI-specific tests
Test that valid args for each command parse correctly
Test that missing required args (e.g. --season) produce descriptive errors
Test that invalid values (e.g. --season abc) produce descriptive errors
Test format flag resolution (--json, --csv, --format)
All tests pass via npm run test

userStories.title: Formatter unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for all three formatters to ensure output is correct.
acceptanceCriteria: Test JSON formatter outputs valid pretty-printed JSON
Test CSV formatter outputs correct headers and properly escapes special characters
Test table formatter truncates to given width and pads columns
Test table formatter respects --full vs default column subset
All tests pass via npm run test

userStories.title: Compiled binary and final quality pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the compiled binary to work and all quality checks to pass.
acceptanceCriteria: bun build --compile src/cli.ts produces a working standalone binary
All existing library tests continue to pass
npm run typecheck passes
npm run check passes
npm run test passes (including new CLI tests)
